heading: Board of Examiners
label: 3931 Board of Examiners
notes: Establishment of Board of ExaminersFor establishment of the Board of Examiners for the Foreign Service and appointment of its members, see section 6 of Ex. Ord. No. 12293, , 46 F.R. 13970, set out as note under .Feb. 23, 1981section 3901 of this title

children.heading: Establishment; membership; chairman
children.label: a Establishment; membership; chairman
children.text: The President shall establish a Board of Examiners for the Foreign Service to develop, and supervise the administration of, examinations prescribed under to be given to candidates for appointment in the Service. The Board shall consist of 15 members appointed by the President (no fewer than 5 of whom shall be appointed from among individuals who are not Government employees and who shall be qualified for service on the Board by virtue of their knowledge, experience, or training in the fields of testing or equal employment opportunity). The Board shall include representatives of agencies utilizing the Foreign Service personnel system and representatives of other agencies which have responsibility for employment testing. The Board shall be chaired by a member of the Board, designated by the President, who is a member of the Service.section 3941(b) of this title

children.heading: Review of examinations; report to Secretary of State
children.label: b Review of examinations; report to Secretary of State

children.text: whether any such examination has an adverse impact on the hiring, promotion, or other employment opportunity of members of any race, sex, or ethnic group;
methods of minimizing any such adverse impact;
alternatives to any examinations which have such an adverse impact; and
whether such examinations are valid in relation to job performance.

children.heading: Vacancies
children.label: c Vacancies
children.text: Any vacancy or vacancies on the Board shall not impair the right of the remaining members to exercise the full powers of the Board.
